main_event: 15-year-old amateur astronomer in Newman observed round object with red and green flashing lights and halo for 5 minutes on 27 February 1967.
sighted_object: round shaped object with red and green lights flashing; halo flashed on and off irregularly, outlining metal skin of object
witness_description: civilian male age 15, amateur astronomer, observed from inside building through window then went outdoors
interesting_points: Initially observed through window glass from inside, then went outside to verify not a reflection
Red and green lights flashing on object with constantly shifting positions
Halo flashed on and off irregularly; when on, stayed for about 2 to 3 seconds outlining and showing metal skin distinctly
Observer is amateur astronomer and stated sighting bore no resemblance to aircraft, planetary movements, satellites, or meteors
Object about 1/3 covered by match head at arm's length
Initial letter delayed due to search for other witnesses
